The [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] '''Royal crusaders''' were the elite members of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]. In battle, they rode large, powerful [[Guarlara/Legends|guarlara]] steeds and charged into the heart of enemy [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]]. They employed powerful [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]] rather than [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] and were outfitted at [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|Security Force]] [[Fortress/Legends|fortresses]]. Crusaders were typically equipped for attacking [[Mech/Legends|mechanized units]], [[Autoturret/Legends|turrets]], and [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], but were vulnerable against [[Starship/Legends|aircraft]] and [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-bearing [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref name="Prima" /> In addition to the crusaders, the even more well-equipped [[Elite Royal Crusader]]s could deal more powerful blows to enemy forces on the battlefield and were protected by [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]].<ref name="GB" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], seven crusaders were used by the Royal Naboo Security Forces to [[Capture of Harte Secur|defend]] the Naboo [[City/Legends|settlement]] of [[Harte Secur]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] megacorporation. The crusaders were [[Death/Legends|killed]], however, and the Federation conquered the city. Four crusaders loyal to the [[Naboo Royal Navy]] were also present when the Trade Federation [[Invasion of Spinnaker|invaded]] the city [[Spinnaker/Legends|Spinnaker]]. They attacked a Federation-occupied [[Unidentified Naboo village (OOM-9)|village]] but were defeated.<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Crusaders received their first mention in the [[Prima Games|Prima]] [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] to the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', where they were incorrectly stated to cost 100 units of food.<ref name="Prima" /> They later appeared in the game during the second Trade Federation mission, titled "Behind the Lines," and exclusively in the hard difficulty mode of the third level, "Spinnaker's Spoils."<ref name="GB" />

Royal crusaders are the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo civilization's]] unique units that are produced at fortresses for a cost of 150 units of food and eighty units of [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]].<ref name="GB" /> They are stronger than many of the game's other melee units.<ref name="Prima" /> Among enhancements made by the developers for the [[2002]] expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']], Royal Crusaders became cheaper to produce<ref name="GB2">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' manual</ref>—now costing 125 food and sixty nova crystals<ref name="GBCC">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref>—gaining greater movement speed, and doing more damage to [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] and walls.<ref name="GB2" />
